description: §52.15.5 (S255) round-3 defect 4 — a PUBLIC (auth=none) callable-init cell coalesced with a GATED one is NOT swept into the gated group: it stays SSR-seeded (its rows are server-rendered into the first paint + carried in window.__scrml_ssr_state), while the gated @secretCell is omitted (client-hydrated behind its per-cell /__mountHydrate gate). The E-ADAPTER SSR path composes the anon first-paint: @publicCell rows are PRESENT, @secretCell rows are ABSENT. The I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED info lint fires only on the gated @secretCell. (Round-2 gated + omitted the WHOLE callable group all-or-nothing → the public cell never hydrated for anon; the per-cell omission fixes that.)
rationale: §52.15.5: the SSR seed is omitted PER CELL — a gated callable cell is omitted, a public callable cell stays seeded. The coalesced /__mountHydrate route gates each cell by its OWN auth (public always served, gated only to the authorized), never collapsing to one route gate.
